enriched_points.expanded_content: Traditional urban drainage systems primarily focus on rapidly channeling stormwater away from urban areas, using pipes and culverts to prevent flooding.
These systems often suffer from aging infrastructure, inadequate capacity, and poor maintenance, leading to frequent flooding and water quality issues.
Conventional approaches prioritize quick water removal, neglecting natural absorption and filtration methods, which can exacerbate urban flooding and pollution.
